steps.title: Find the angle at S
steps.content:
In triangle $QXS$, the given angles are $48^\circ$ and $86^\circ$. Let $q$ be the remaining angle:
$$q=180^\circ-48^\circ-86^\circ=46^\circ$$

steps.title: Find the angle at W
steps.content:
At $W$, the given $85^\circ$ angle and the angle inside triangle $RWS$ form a linear pair. Let $w$ be that angle:
$$w=180^\circ-85^\circ=95^\circ$$

steps.title: Find the angle at R
steps.content:
Use triangle $RWS$ with angles $46^\circ$ and $95^\circ$. Let $r$ be the angle at $R$:
$$r=180^\circ-95^\circ-46^\circ=39^\circ$$
Because the same straight lines continue through $R$ and $T$, this gives the angle at $R$ in triangle $RTU$.

steps.title: Finish triangle RTU
steps.content:
Since the $162^\circ$ angle and the angle at $T$ in triangle $RTU$ are supplementary, the angle at $T$ is:
$$180^\circ-162^\circ=18^\circ$$
Then the requested angle is:
$$180^\circ-39^\circ-18^\circ=123^\circ$$
The answer is 123.
